entries.title: Ministry denies Voldemort until public sighting

content.fact: Throughout most of Year 5, the Ministry denies Voldemort’s return and treats Harry/Dumbledore as politically inconvenient or unstable.
content.injection: Before Voldemort is publicly seen at the Ministry, keep official institutions in denial or suppression mode; do not write the Ministry as openly mobilized against Voldemort yet.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
If the active Context is before this window, block this state unless accepted story lore explicitly changed it.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Before Voldemort is publicly seen; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not import later-book knowledge, deaths, relationships, or reveals into this Context.

context.label: Umbridge classroom control before DA exposure

content.fact: Umbridge’s Defense class suppresses practical defensive magic, creating the need for Dumbledore’s Army.
content.injection: During Umbridge’s control, do not give students normal practical Defense instruction from her; the DA exists because formal class is politically constrained and practically useless.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
If the active Context is before this window, block this state unless accepted story lore explicitly changed it.

entries.title: DA stays secret before Marietta betrayal

content.fact: The DA is a clandestine student training group and must stay hidden from Umbridge until Marietta’s betrayal and the official crackdown.
content.injection: Before the DA betrayal, do not have Umbridge or the school administration casually know the DA’s membership, meeting room, or practice details unless a scene establishes discovery.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.

entries.title: Full prophecy unknown until Dumbledore explains

content.fact: Before the end of Year 5, Harry has visions and fragments but not Dumbledore’s full explanation of the prophecy and its implications.
content.injection: Before Dumbledore explains the prophecy, block full prophecy exposition, Chosen One framing, and confident prophecy logic from Harry’s internal reasoning or ordinary dialogue.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.

content.fact: Sirius is alive, trapped in Grimmauld Place, and emotionally volatile until the Department of Mysteries battle.
content.injection: Before the veil event, do not treat Sirius as dead or posthumous; after the death anchor, switch to grief/absence cards and block casual living Sirius appearances unless AU is declared.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
If the active Context is before this window, block this state unless accepted story lore explicitly changed it.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Before Sirius dies; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.

content.fact: Harry receives Occlumency lessons from Snape but does not master them, leaving him vulnerable to visions and manipulation.
content.injection: During the Occlumency window, do not make Harry reliably shielded or skilled at Occlumency; his failure is part of why the false Sirius vision works. Block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
